mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and rising capacity utilization, which drives upward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55 percent of periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.5 percent signals a tight labor market that accelerates wage-cost pressures and prompts additional Federal Reserve tightening, which reduces equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months when UNRATE is below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in the federal funds rate and a consumer price index level above 190 indicates restrictive monetary policy acting on elevated inflation, which raises real discount rates and squeezes profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between the interaction signal and 60-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, month-over-month growth in retail sales above 2 percent signals resilient consumer demand that invites further Fed rate increases, which de-rates equities and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of triggered instances during identified tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting labor income and weakening aggregate demand, which triggers a rapid risk-off repricing and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 45 percent of months with negative payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly acceleration in real GDP signals broad-based final demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue growth expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and 60-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index level above 190 indicates elevated inflation expectations that increase required real equity returns and compress valuation multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 190.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is below 5.5 percent and the federal funds rate is rising, the joint pressure of tight labor markets and active monetary tightening accelerates discount-rate compression and margin contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of instances where both UNRATE is below 5.5 and FEDFUNDS is rising.
